description: Regular team meeting vs project kickoff same morning - should NOT match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Phoenix Project Kickoff
- Start: 2026-07-08T10:00:00
- End: 2026-07-08T12:00:00
- Location: Large Conference Room
- Description: Initial kickoff for Phoenix redesign project. All stakeholders required.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: Weekly Team Sync
   - Start: 2026-07-08T09:00:00
   - End: 2026-07-08T09:30:00
   - Location: Conference Room B
   - Description: Regular Tuesday morning team standup

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: 1:1 with Manager
   - Start: 2026-07-08T14:00:00
   - End: 2026-07-08T14:30:00
   - Location: Manager's Office
   - Description: Bi-weekly check-in

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Code Review
   - Start: 2026-07-08T15:00:00
   - End: 2026-07-08T16:00:00
   - Location: Dev Room
   - Description: Review PRs for authentication module

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: { "matched_event_id": null, "reasoning": "The new event (Phoenix Project Kickoff) has a different time (10:00-12:00) and location (Large Conference Room) than all existing events on the same date. The titles and descriptions also do not match, indicating they are distinct events." }
